product_overview: CarbonScopes is a backend API platform for carbon accounting, providing authentication, carbon data calculation, agent orchestration, sandboxes, knowledge bases, and programmatic access via API keys and webhooks.
core_goals: Provide secure, rate-limited authentication (magic-link OTP + Google OAuth) and account initialization flows
Expose programmatic access via API keys and authenticated endpoints for carbon footprint calculation and project summaries
Support AI agent orchestration and conversational threads for automating carbon analysis tasks
Offer sandboxed environments and knowledge base management for agents to operate safely
Protect the platform using CSRF tokens and rate limiting while supporting webhook integrations

features.name: Health Check API
features.description: Simple health endpoints to report service liveness and basic status.
user_flows: GET /healthz -> Receive 200 with {"status":"ok"}
GET /v1/health -> Receive 200 with {"status":"ok"}
GET /v1/health -> Receive 200 with {"status":"ok"} (note: may report healthy even when the database is unreachable - known limitation)

features.name: Authentication API (OTP magic link)
features.description: Send OTP magic links to user email and verify tokens to issue access credentials (supports rate limiting).
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/otp/send with body {"email":"user@example.com"} -> Receive 200 OTPResponse (magic link / email sent)
POST /v1/auth/otp/verify with body {"token":"<otp-token>"} -> Receive 200 OTPResponse with access JWT
POST /v1/auth/otp/send with body {"email":"user@example.com"} repeated more than configured limit (5 per 15 minutes) -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded
POST /v1/auth/otp/verify with body {"token":"invalid-or-expired"} -> Receive 400 or 401 invalid/expired token error

features.name: CSRF Protection
features.description: Double-submit cookie CSRF protection with a token endpoint and header validation for cookie-based sessions.
user_flows: GET /v1/csrf-token -> Receive 200 with csrf_token cookie set
POST /v1/threads with X-CSRF-Token header matching csrf_token cookie and session cookie present -> Receive 201 Thread created
POST /v1/threads without X-CSRF-Token header but cookie present -> Receive 403 Forbidden (CSRF validation failed)
GET /v1/csrf-token -> Receive 200 with csrf_token cookie set (note: cookie secure flag may block HTTP local development - known limitation)

features.name: Rate Limiting
features.description: Protect sensitive endpoints (auth, API key creation, webhooks) with configurable limits and Redis-backed storage with in-memory fallback.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/otp/send with body {"email":"user@example.com"} -> Receive 200 OTPResponse (within limit)
POST /v1/auth/otp/send with body {"email":"user@example.com"} repeated 6 times from same client within 15 minutes -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded
POST /v1/webhooks/github with payload -> Receive 200 OK (within webhook rate limits)
POST /v1/webhooks/github repeated rapidly beyond configured webhook limit -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: API Key Management
features.description: Create, list, revoke, and delete API keys for programmatic access to the platform.
user_flows: POST /v1/api-keys with Bearer token (user authenticated) -> Receive 200 APIKeyCreateResponse with new API key value
GET /v1/api-keys with Bearer token -> Receive 200 list of APIKeyResponse entries including newly created key
PATCH /v1/api-keys/{key_id}/revoke with Bearer token -> Receive 200 APIKeyResponse with revoked status
POST /v1/api-keys without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Account Setup
features.description: Initialize authenticated and anonymous user accounts for first-time use.
user_flows: POST /v1/initialize with Bearer token (authenticated user) and setup payload -> Receive 200 account initialized
POST /v1/initialize-anonymous with body {"display_name":"Guest"} -> Receive 200 anonymous account created
POST /v1/initialize-anonymous repeated more than allowed rate -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded
POST /v1/initialize without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Carbon Data Management
features.description: Load TGO data, calculate carbon footprints, and retrieve project-level carbon summaries and certifications.
user_flows: POST /v1/tgo/load with Bearer token and valid TGO payload -> Receive 200 load confirmation
POST /v1/carbon/calculate with Bearer token and valid data -> Receive 200 with carbon calculation results
POST /v1/project/{project_id}/summary with Bearer token -> Receive 200 project carbon summary
GET /v1/certify/edge -> Receive 200 certification edge data
POST /v1/carbon/calculate with malformed or incomplete payload -> Receive 400 Bad Request
GET /v1/project/{project_id}/summary without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Agent Management
features.description: Create, run, monitor, and stop AI agent runs for automated workflows.
user_flows: POST /v1/agent/start with Bearer token and valid agent config -> Receive 200 with {"agent_run_id":"<id>"}
GET /v1/agent-runs/{agent_run_id}/status with Bearer token -> Receive 200 with run status (running/completed/failed)
POST /v1/agent-run/{agent_run_id}/stop with Bearer token -> Receive 200 stop confirmation
GET /v1/agent-runs/active with Bearer token -> Receive 200 list of active runs
POST /v1/agent/start without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Thread Management
features.description: Manage conversational threads used by agents and users to track dialog state.
user_flows: POST /v1/threads with Bearer token and body {"title":"New Thread"} -> Receive 201 with thread_id
GET /v1/threads with Bearer token -> Receive 200 with list of threads
POST /v1/threads without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Sandbox Management
features.description: Create and operate code sandboxes: file operations and terminal execution within project-scoped sandboxes.
user_flows: POST /v1/project/{project_id}/sandbox/ensure-active with Bearer token -> Receive 200 with {"sandbox_id":"<id>", "status":"active"}
POST /v1/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/files with Bearer token and file payload -> Receive 201 file created
GET /v1/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/files with Bearer token -> Receive 200 list of files
POST /v1/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/terminal/execute with Bearer token and {"command":"ls"} -> Receive 200 with command output
POST /v1/sandboxes/{invalid_sandbox_id}/terminal/execute with Bearer token -> Receive 404 Not Found

features.name: Knowledge Base
features.description: Manage folders and upload files used by agents as knowledge bases.
user_flows: POST /v1/folders with Bearer token and body {"name":"Project KB"} -> Receive 201 folder created with folder_id
GET /v1/folders with Bearer token -> Receive 200 list of folders
POST /v1/folders/{folder_id}/upload with Bearer token and file payload -> Receive 200 upload success
POST /v1/folders/{folder_id}/upload without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Webhook System
features.description: Receive external webhook payloads for integrations (rate-limited public endpoints).
user_flows: POST /v1/webhooks/github with JSON payload -> Receive 200 OK (webhook accepted)
POST /v1/webhooks/stripe with JSON payload -> Receive 200 OK (webhook accepted)
POST /v1/webhooks/github repeated beyond allowed rate window -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: Chat API
features.description: Primary chat entry point for agent conversations and prompts.
user_flows: POST /v1/chat with Bearer token and body {"prompt":"Calculate emissions for X"} -> Receive 200 with agent response
POST /v1/chat without Bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /v1/chat with Bearer token but exceeding chat rate limits -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

known_limitations.issue: CSRF cookie secure flag defaults to true, may block local development over HTTP
known_limitations.impact: CSRF token cookie may not be set in local HTTP development

known_limitations.issue: Rate limiter falls back to in-memory storage when Redis unavailable
known_limitations.impact: Rate limits reset on server restart in single-instance deployments

known_limitations.issue: Anonymous account initialization has no CAPTCHA protection
known_limitations.impact: Potential for automated anonymous account creation

known_limitations.issue: Health check endpoint does not verify database connectivity
known_limitations.impact: Health check may report healthy when database is down
